talk_metadata.session_title: The Dharma Master Yantai Advances in Targeted Therapy
talk_metadata.talk_title: Revisiting the Neoantigen Approach to Cancer Immunotherapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "most neoantigens derive from passenger mutations, and they're not recurrent across patients"
Speaker said: "neoantigens are often subclonal, making them ineffective targets"
Speaker said: "especially in low TMB tumors, you will not have sufficient neoantigens"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: subclonal distribution limits effective TCR recognition and enables immune evasion
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "neoantigens are often subclonal, making them easy targets for immunoevasion"

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy data presented
No clinical trial data or patient outcomes

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify patients with low TMB who could benefit from translation‑fidelity inhibition to expand neoantigen repertoire
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "especially in low TMB tumors, you will not have sufficient neoantigens"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict which low‑TMB tumors will generate high‑affinity neoantigens after translation‑fidelity perturbation

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the subclonal neoantigen problem by using CrisPRO to prioritize clonal neoantigens derived from driver mutations
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "most neoantigens derive from passenger mutations, and they're not recurrent across patients"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine can rank recurrent driver‑derived neoantigens for vaccine or TCR design

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine translation‑fidelity inhibitors (e.g., TYW2 knockout) with checkpoint blockade to overcome subclonal resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed synergy between chemotherapy‑induced neoantigen presentation and anti‑PD‑1 in mouse models
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of combination regimens on neoantigen load and immune activation

unresolved_questions: How to reliably convert subclonal passenger neoantigens into clonal therapeutic targets?
What is the optimal timing and dosing of translation‑fidelity inhibition to maximize immunogenicity without toxicity?

talk_metadata.session_title: The Dharma Master Yantai Advances in Targeted Therapy
talk_metadata.talk_title: Pharmacologic Inhibition of PMS2 to Induce Mismatch Repair Deficiency
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the molecule recapitulates perfectly what we do genetically"
Speaker said: "the transition is much faster into the MSI status high tumor mutational burden"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: lack of in‑vivo pharmacokinetic properties limits translation to clinic
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker said: "some patients did have a prolonged response after increased mutational burden"
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "the molecule is non‑toxic" but also noted it is not yet suitable for in‑vivo use

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Induction of mismatch repair deficiency via temozolomide priming
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker‑driven selection of patients likely to achieve sufficient mutational burden
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Predictive marker for successful conversion to MSI‑high status
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Underpowered to detect benefit in heterogeneous patient population

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Binding relies on a single cysteine (C73); potential for resistance via cysteine mutation
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad use limited by need for combination with chemotherapy and lack of oral formulation

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TYW2 knockout increases neoantigen presentation, then concluded that inhibition is safe because TYW2 knockout mice are non‑pathological – ignoring potential long‑term autoimmunity

key_data_gaps: Limited clinical efficacy data (ARETUSA trial negative endpoint)

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO to design next‑generation PMS2 inhibitors that avoid cysteine‑73 resistance mutations
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "binding relies on a cysteine, C73, and no other cysteine is targeted"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model covalent binding landscapes and predict alternative pocket residues for robust inhibitor design

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify optimal chemotherapy partners (e.g., platinum agents) that synergize with PMS2 inhibition to accelerate MSI conversion
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "combination of FOLFOXIRI and 1867 shows much faster transition into MSI high"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate mutational signature acceleration under different drug combos to prioritize trials

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop predictive biomarkers (e.g., baseline TYW2 expression) to select patients likely to respond to PMS2 inhibition
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "low TYW2 is correlated with better response to immune checkpoint blockade"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate expression data with mutational modeling to define responder cohorts

cited_competitors.name: Neophore
cited_competitors.context: collaborators that co‑developed the PMS2 inhibitor

unresolved_questions: Can an oral formulation of the PMS2 inhibitor achieve sufficient tumor exposure?
What is the minimal duration of PMS2 inhibition needed to generate a durable MSI‑high phenotype?

talk_metadata.session_title: The Dharma Master Yantai Advances in Targeted Therapy
talk_metadata.talk_title: Chemically Engineering Mismatch Repair Deficiency to Induce Tumor Immunogenicity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "temozolomide and cisplatin induce a high increase in TMB and MSI score"
Speaker said: "the combination leads to a massively subclonal tumor but still highly immunogenic"
Speaker said: "the trial did not meet its primary endpoint, but patients with high TMB gain and MSI gain survived longer"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: subclonal mutation landscape may limit durable immune control in some patients
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "we didn't reach our primary endpoint, which was primarily due to response"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker said: "patients that did the best had high TMB gain and MSI gain"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Induction of mismatch repair deficiency via temozolomide + cisplatin
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled refractory colorectal cancer patients without pre‑selection for likelihood of MMR conversion
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline MSH2 promoter methylation status to predict conversion
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA monitoring to track dynamic MSI conversion
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Underpowered to detect benefit in heterogeneous cohort

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on non‑specific DNA damage; other groups can replicate with same agents
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Toxicity of temozolomide and cisplatin limits dose intensity and patient tolerance

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that subclonal frameshift mutations are highly immunogenic in mice, then concluded that clonality is not required for clinical efficacy – contradicting conventional wisdom that clonal neoantigens are needed

key_data_gaps: No clear biomarker to predict which patients will convert to MSI‑high

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to predict which tumors will undergo rapid MSH2 promoter methylation after temozolomide/cisplatin exposure
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we saw a lot of methylation in MSH2 promoter, which induced lack of expression"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate epigenetic modeling with mutational signature prediction to select responders

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify less toxic platinum analogs (e.g., carboplatin) that synergize with temozolomide to induce MSI without excessive toxicity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker asked: "can this be recapitulated with carboplatin or oxaliplatin?"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate DNA adduct formation and downstream mutational signatures for different platinums

crispro_opportunity.description: Demonstrate that CrisPRO‑guided induction of specific indel signatures outperforms random chemotherapy‑induced mutagenesis
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the combination leads to a massively subclonal tumor but still highly immunogenic"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can design targeted mutagenic interventions that generate high‑affinity, immunogenic frameshifts with minimal off‑target effects

cited_competitors.name: Alberto's PMS2 inhibitor program (Knyohfor)
cited_competitors.context: alternative pharmacologic way to induce MMR deficiency

unresolved_questions: Why do some patients fail to acquire MSI‑high status despite the same drug exposure?
What is the optimal dosing schedule to maximize mutational load while minimizing normal tissue toxicity?
